req.title: Search patients (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-088 mandates three orthogonal concerns: (1) correct patient-scoping — only care-team patients returned; (2) audit emission — an AuditEvent must exist after every search; (3) input validation — malformed queries yield HTTP 400. EP drives the happy-path search by each supported param (name/MRN/DOB). BVA targets the single-character name query, the minimal valid input boundary. NEG covers the malformed query string that must produce 400. DT would cover multi-param combinations; STC covers the state of AuditEvent records growing monotonically. To stay within the 5-test budget while honouring the 3-minimum rule, I include: positive/EP (name search + audit), boundary/BVA (single-char name), and negative/NEG (malformed query). Each test is idempotent: fixtures create and tear down their own data; audit checks use a count delta rather than a global assertion.

tests.name: test_search_by_name_returns_scoped_patients_and_emits_audit
preconditions: At least one patient assigned to the clinician exists (seeded by <patient_fixture>)
At least one patient NOT assigned to the clinician exists (seeded by <other_patient_fixture>)
actions: Record current AuditEvent count for this clinician via GET /AuditEvent
expected: Response is a FHIR Bundle with resourceType=='Bundle'
All returned Patient resources are in the clinician's care team
Out-of-scope patient is NOT present in the bundle
New AuditEvent.entity references the search query
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_search_by_name_returns_scoped_patients_and_emits_audit(
    base_url, clinician_headers, seeded_patient, other_patient, audit_count
):
    family = seeded_patient["name"][0]["family"]
    scoped_id = seeded_patient["id"]
    alien_id = other_patient["id"]

    before = audit_count(clinician_headers)

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        params={"name": family},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"

    ids = [e["resource"]["id"] for e in bundle.get("entry", [])]
    assert scoped_id in ids, "Care-team patient missing from results"
    assert alien_id not in ids, "Out-of-scope patient leaked"

    after = audit_count(clinician_headers)
    assert after - before == 1, "Expected exactly one new AuditEvent"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"agent": clinician_headers.get("X-Clinician-Id"), "_sort": "-date", "_count": 1},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    latest = audit_resp.json()["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert latest["action"] in ("E", "R"), "AuditEvent action unexpected"
    query_text = str(latest.get("entity", []))
    assert family in query_text or "Patient" in query_text

tests.name: test_search_by_single_character_name_boundary
preconditions: Patient with family name starting with 'A' assigned to clinician (seeded by <patient_fixture>)
actions: GET /Patient?name=A with clinician Bearer token (single character — minimum valid input boundary)
expected: HTTP 200 returned (single-char is valid per FHIR spec prefix match)
Bundle contains at least the seeded patient whose name starts with 'A'
trace: R-HLT-088: search by name — boundary at minimum query length
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_search_by_single_character_name_boundary(
    base_url, clinician_headers, seeded_patient_name_a, audit_count
):
    scoped_id = seeded_patient_name_a["id"]

    before = audit_count(clinician_headers)

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        params={"name": "A"},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"

    ids = [e["resource"]["id"] for e in bundle.get("entry", [])]
    assert scoped_id in ids, "Boundary patient with name starting 'A' not returned"

    for entry in bundle.get("entry", []):
        pid = entry["resource"]["id"]
        assert pid in <clinician_care_team_ids>(clinician_headers, base_url), \
            f"Out-of-scope patient {pid} returned"

    after = audit_count(clinician_headers)
    assert after - before == 1, "AuditEvent not emitted for single-char search"

tests.name: test_malformed_search_query_returns_400
actions: GET /Patient?search=%%INVALID%% with clinician Bearer token (percent-encoding error makes query malformed)
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome with issue severity 'error'
AuditEvent is still emitted (audit on failure is required by R-HLT-088)
AuditEvent outcome code indicates failure (e.g. '8' or 'error')
trace: R-HLT-088: error pathway — 400 when query malformed
R-HLT-088: access logged even on error
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_malformed_search_query_returns_400(
    base_url, clinician_headers, audit_count
):
    before = audit_count(clinician_headers)

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Patient",
        params={"name": "%%INVALID%%"},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400 for malformed query, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", \
        "400 body must be OperationOutcome"
    severities = [i["severity"] for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert "error" in severities or "fatal" in severities

    after = audit_count(clinician_headers)
    assert after - before == 1, "AuditEvent must be emitted even for failed searches"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"agent": clinician_headers.get("X-Clinician-Id"), "_sort": "-date", "_count": 1},
        headers=clinician_headers,
    )
    latest = audit_resp.json()["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert latest.get("outcome") in ("4", "8", "12", "error"), \
        "AuditEvent outcome must reflect search failure"
